channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Father and son Ashley and Tyler Young could face each other in the FA Cup
item.description: Former England international Ashley Young could face his 18-year-old son Tyler when Everton hosts Peterborough in the third round of the FA Cup.
item.dc:creator: James Robson, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Sinner, Djokovic in opposite halves at the Australian Open, Sabalenka vs Stephens in 1st round
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — Defending champion Jannik Sinner and 10-time Australian Open winner Novak Djokovic have landed in opposite sides of the draw for the season’s first major, ruling out a replay of last year’s semifinal match.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Independent streak helped build Notre Dame into football's historic and now modern behemoth
item.description: It was some combination of religion, a grudge involving Knute Rockne and a rival, and maybe the simple time-honored notion that some people just don’t like Notre Dame.
item.dc:creator: Eddie Pells, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Inside the numbers of Cleveland's 129-122 win over Oklahoma City
item.description: CLEVELAND (AP) — The most-hyped game so far this season in the NBA didn't disappoint. Cleveland beat Oklahoma City 129-122 on Wednesday night, with the Cavaliers — now winners of 11 straight — pushing the NBA's best record to 32-4 and snapping the Thunder's 15-game winning streak.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pro Picks: Texans, Ravens, Bills, Eagles, Bucs and Vikings will advance to the next round
item.description: The NFL’s wild-card weekend features divisional rivals facing off, two NFC rematches from Week 1 and the Harbaugh brothers coaching on the same day. Jim Harbaugh leads the Chargers against the Texans in the season’s first playoff game on Saturday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: ROB MAADDI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Clippers' Kawhi Leonard leaves team to help family deal with raging wildfires: report
item.description: Los Angeles Clippers star Kawhi Leonard will reportedly leave the team to tend to his family dealing with the wildfires raging across the city.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 49ers’ Shanahan spins his coordinator carousel, Lynch sums up 6-11 trainwreck
item.description: Coach Kyle Shanahan and general manager John Lynch huddled with the media Wednesday to review this season's 6-11 last-place finish and swift end to their NFC reign.
item.dc:creator: Cam Inman, The Mercury News

item.title: NHL postpones LA Kings' home game against Calgary due to wildfires while NFL, NBA monitor situation
item.description: The NHL postponed the Los Angeles Kings’ home game against the Calgary Flames on Wednesday with several massive wildfires still burning across the greater Los Angeles area.
item.dc:creator: Greg Beacham, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: UCLA's Mick Cronin calls his players 'soft' and 'delusional' after losing for 3rd time in 4 games
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — UCLA coach Mick Cronin ripped into his players following the 22nd-ranked Bruins' third loss in four games, saying they don't give enough effort and lack toughness.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Thune tees up Tuberville's bill to block transgender athletes from women’s sports
item.description: Senate Majority Leader John Thune (R-S.D.) on Tuesday took the necessary procedural steps to place Sen. Tommy Tuberville’s (R-Ala.) bill blocking transgender athletes from women’s sports on the Senate calendar. The step sets up a vote on Tuberville’s bill in the weeks ahead.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Former Baltimore Orioles left-hander Brian Matusz, who made final MLB appearance with Cubs in 2016, dies at 37
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — Brian Matusz, the left-handed pitcher who spent eight seasons in the major leagues and made his final big-league appearance with the Cubs during their 2016 World Series championship season, has died. He was 37. Matusz, a former No.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Masters gives another invitation to Niemann. Hojgaard invitation means Augusta gets twins
item.description: Joaquin Niemann of LIV Golf is returning to the Masters, the second straight time Augusta National has given the Chilean a special invitation.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FERGUSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Heisman Trophy runner-up Ashton Jeanty of Boise State to enter NFL draft
item.description: BOISE, Idaho (AP) — Ashton Jeanty, who rushed for the second-most yards in a season in major-college football history and was runner-up for the Heisman Trophy, will leave Boise State to enter the NFL draft.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Report: Bears denied permission by Cowboys to interview Mike McCarthy for head coaching job
item.description: CHICAGO — The Bears are casting a wide net in their search for the 18th head coach in franchise history as a critical offseason for the team gets underway. One potential candidate will have to be taken off the interview list — at least for the next several days, if not for good.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: Final chapter: France's greatest coach Didier Deschamps says he'll leave after 2026 World Cup
item.description: Didier Deschamps announced Wednesday that he won’t continue as France coach after the next World Cup, bringing an end to an era in which he became the nation's most successful manager over more than a decade.
item.dc:creator: SAMUEL PETREQUIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trae Young's 49-foot buzzer-beater lifts Hawks to a 124-121 win over the Jazz
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (AP) — Trae Young hit a buzzer-beating 3-pointer from beyond the halfcourt line to give the Atlanta Hawks a 124-121 victory over the Utah Jazz on Tuesday night.
item.dc:creator: JOHN COON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Brayden Point scores with 52 seconds to play to lift Lightning to 3-2 win over Hurricanes
item.description: Brayden Point scored with 52 seconds to play and the Tampa Bay Lightning snapped a four-game skid with a 3-2 victory over the Carolina Hurricanes on Tuesday night.
item.dc:creator: ERIK ERLENDSSON Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Giants agree to deal with Justin Verlander: reports
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (KRON) -- The San Francisco Giants are adding former MVP Justin Verlander to their pitching staff, multiple outlets reported Tuesday. The team reportedly agreed to a one-year deal with the pitcher. Verlander, 41, is one of the best pitchers of the 2000s and a future Hall of Famer.
item.dc:creator: Phil Mayer, KRON4

item.title: CMU Women's wrestler Marissa Gallegos reflects on wrestling journey
item.description: GRAND JUNCTION, Colo. (KREX)—Marissa Gallegos is no stranger to the spotlight. The Colorado Mesa University star has cemented her legacy as the winningest women’s wrestler in program history. Her career, defined by grit, dominance, and determination, has inspired athletes across the sport.
item.dc:creator: Titus Cleveland, KREX 5

item.title: Column: Bears need leaders on the line alongside Caleb Williams in 2025
item.description: CHICAGO (WGN) — While the 2024 season was every single bit of a disaster, the Chicago Bears' win over the Green Bay Packers at Lambeau Field Sunday provided a glimmer of hope. It could also double as a springboard into a pivotal offseason.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: 49ers stars reflect on how season badly derailed
item.description: SANTA CLARA, Calif. (KRON) -- As the San Francisco 49ers packed up their lockers at Levi's Stadium on Monday, players told reporters about how they will move forward into next season. Tight end George Kittle summed up the season as "disappointing.
item.dc:creator: Amy Larson, KRON4

item.title: Los Angeles Dodgers agree to trade Gavin Lux to Reds: report
item.description: The post World Series shakeup continues in Los Angeles, as the Dodgers are poised to trade starting second-baseman Gavin Lux to the Cincinnati Reds. As reported by ESPN's Jeff Passan, the Dodgers will send Lux to the Reds in exchange for a future draft pick and outfield prospect Mike Sirota.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KTLA 5

item.title: North Central football team wins third Division III national title, finishes season a perfect 15-0
item.description: HOUSTON (AP) — The North Central football dynasty has reached rarified air. So has quarterback Luke Lehnen.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: Lions secure NFC's No. 1 seed, Broncos and Buccaneers clinch the NFL's final two playoff spots
item.description: The road to the Super Bowl in the NFC will go through Motown. The Detroit Lions secured a No. 1 seed with a 31-9 victory over the Minnesota Vikings on Sunday night.
item.dc:creator: ROB MAADDI, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Colts owner Jim Irsay says he's retaining GM Chris Ballard and coach Shane Steichen
item.description: About four hours after the Colts completed their season with a 26-23 overtime victory over the Jacksonville Jaguars, Irsay ended any speculation about possible offseason changes by announcing in a letter to fans that longtime general manager Chris Ballard and second-year coach Shane Steichen would r
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL MAROT, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Patriots fire coach Jerod Mayo shortly after beating Bills to finish his lone season at 4-13
item.description: FOXBOROUGH, Mass. (AP) — The New England Patriots fired coach Jerod Mayo on Sunday after one season, beginning another reboot for the franchise just a year after it parted with longtime coach Bill Belichick.
item.dc:creator: KYLE HIGHTOWER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Mariota leads playoff-bound Commanders to 23-19 win over Cowboys after replacing Daniels
item.description: Marcus Mariota threw a 5-yard touchdown pass to Terry McLaurin with 3 seconds left, lifting Washington to a 23-19 victory over the Dallas Cowboys on Sunday and clinching the sixth seed in the NFC playoffs for the Commanders.
item.dc:creator: SCHUYLER DIXON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Bears vs. Packers: Injuries, inactives, matchups and what to know for the 2024 season finale
item.description: UPDATE: The Bears and Packers have announced their inactives for Sunday's game. See below. GREEN BAY, Wis. — A long, trying 2024 season for the Bears reaches its merciful conclusion Sunday afternoon at Lambeau Field.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: Knicks visit conference foe Chicago Saturday
item.description: New York Knicks (24-11, third in the Eastern Conference) vs. Chicago Bulls (15-19, 10th in the Eastern Conference) Chicago; Saturday, 8 p.m. EST BETMGM SPORTSBOOK LINE: Knicks -5.5; over/under is 235.5 BOTTOM LINE: Chicago takes on New York in Eastern Conference action Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Red Rocks cruise past Utah State in season opener
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - The fifth-ranked Utah gymnastics team kicked off its 50th anniversary season in style, overcoming a slow start to eventually secure a dominant victory over Utah State Friday night at the Huntsman Center.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: BBB provides tips on buying Detroit Lions tickets, avoiding scams
item.description: The Better Business Bureau of West Michigan is providing some tips if you’re looking to buy tickets to the Detroit Lions game on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Meghan Bunchman, NewsNation

item.title: Postponed Sugar Bowl played with enhanced security about 36 hours after terror attack in New Orleans
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Georgia and Notre Dame fans packed a plaza adjacent to the Superdome and enjoyed music under clear skies — under the watch of snipers on rooftops — before filtering into the stadium for Thursday's College Football Playoff quarterfinal at the Sugar Bowl. “It was a lot of fun.
item.dc:creator: BRETT MARTEL, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Big Will's Week 18 NFL Picks
item.description: In this edition of NFL picks, Big Will focuses on the final AFC wildcard spot as three teams fight for the 7-seed.
item.dc:creator: Big Will Levenson, KREX 5

item.title: UIC men's basketball team scores huge win by knocking mid-major power Drake from ranks of unbeaten
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — The Drake men's basketball team walked into Credit Union 1 Arena on Chicago's Near West Side on New Year's Day with no losses this season. They exited with one, courtesy of the University of Illinois Chicago.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9
